info.title: Mapulus API
info.description:
# Mapulus API

Mapulus is a location intelligence platform for Australian geographies. This API
lets you manage maps and locations, look up statistical boundaries, query
demographic data from the ABS Census, and batch-enrich records with geographic context.

## Authentication

All endpoints require a Bearer token:

```
Authorization: Bearer <your_api_key>
```

API keys can be managed from your Mapulus account under **Profile > API Keys**.
Each key can be scoped to specific resources and given an expiration date.
Keep keys secure — never commit them to repositories.

## Base URLs

| Service | Base URL |
|---------|----------|
| Studio  | `https://api.mapulus.com/v1/studio` |
| Data    | `https://api.mapulus.com/v1/data`   |

## Rate limits

Rate limits are per API key. Headers are included on every response:

| Header | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `X-RateLimit-Limit` | Requests allowed per window |
| `X-RateLimit-Remaining` | Requests remaining |
| `X-RateLimit-Reset` | Unix timestamp when the window resets |

---

# Studio endpoints

The **Maps** and **Locations** endpoints let you manage your maps, pins, layers,
and travel boundaries (isochrones).

## Core concepts

- **Maps** — A map is a container that holds one or more layers of locations
- **Layers** — Each map has layers that group locations together. Expand layer details with `expand[]=layer`
- **Locations** — Pins on a map with coordinates, address, and custom attributes
- **Travel contours** — Isochrone boundaries around a location based on drive, cycle, or walk time
- **Custom attributes** — User-defined key/value metadata on locations (e.g. "Location Type": "Store")
- **Nearest search** — Find the closest locations to a point by distance or travel time, with routing profiles (`driving`, `cycling`, `walking`)

## Pagination

List endpoints return paginated results with `pages` metadata:

```json
{
  "object": "list",
  "data": [...],
  "pages": {
    "object": "pages",
    "total_pages": 5,
    "current_page": 1,
    "next_page": 2,
    "prev_page": null
  },
  "total_count": 120
}
```

## Errors

Studio endpoints return errors in the following format:

```json
{
  "errors": {
    "unauthorized": ["You are not authorized to perform this action"]
  }
}
```

Common error keys: `unauthorized` (401), `404` (not found), `UnprocessableEntity` (422),
`TooManyRequests` (429).

---

# Data endpoints

The **Boundaries**, **Demographics**, and **Enrich** endpoints provide
Australian statistical geography lookups, ABS Census data, and batch enrichment.

## Core concepts

- **Boundaries** — Australian statistical geography areas (SA1–SA4, LGA, postcode, suburb, etc.)
- **H3 indices** — Uber's hexagonal grid at resolutions 3–9 for fast spatial lookups
- **Demographics** — Census-derived data attached to boundaries (population, income, housing, etc.)
- **Field selection** — Use `fields` to request specific data (e.g. `summary.population.persons,medians.age`)
- **Topics** — Groups of related fields (e.g. `age`, `income`, `housing`) for convenient data retrieval
- **Boundary IDs** — Use `type:code` format (e.g. `sa2:117011325`, `lga:17200`, `poa:2000`)

### Boundary types

`sa1`, `sa2`, `sa3`, `sa4`, `lga`, `poa`, `sal`, `ced`, `sed`, `gccsa`, `ste`,
`sua`, `ucl`, `ra`, `sos`, `sosr`, `iloc`, `iare`, `ireg`, `flood`

## Demographics queries

The unified `GET /v1/data/demographics` and `POST /v1/data/demographics` endpoints cover all
demographic queries. Combine parameters to express what you need:

| Mode | Parameters | Description |
|------|-----------|-------------|
| Point lookup | `lat`, `lng` | Demographics for the boundary containing a point (default SA1) |
| Single boundary | `boundary_id=sa2:117011325` | Demographics for one boundary |
| Multiple boundaries | `boundary_id=sa1:123,sa1:456` | Individual results or `aggregate=true` |
| Browse by type | `boundary_type=sa2` | Paginated list with `filter`, `sort`, `contained_by` |
| Segment query | `boundary_id` + `topics` + `sex`/`age_min`/`age_max` | Cross-tab breakdown for a single boundary |
| Polygon query (POST) | `geometry` (GeoJSON body) | Boundaries intersecting a polygon, with area-weighted overlap |

### Segment queries

Add `sex`, `age_min`, `age_max` to any single-boundary query to get a cross-tab
breakdown. Requires exactly one `topics` value:

```
GET /v1/data/demographics?boundary_id=sa1:123&topics=income&sex=female&age_min=25&age_max=44
```

### Polygon queries (POST)

Send a GeoJSON polygon in the request body. Add `aggregate=true` for area-weighted totals:

```json
POST /v1/data/demographics
{"geometry": {"type": "Polygon", "coordinates": [...]},
 "boundary_type": "sa2", "topics": "income", "aggregate": true}
```

## Filtering

Use the `filter` parameter on data list endpoints to filter by field values:

| Operator | Example | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| `eq` | `summary.population.persons.eq.10000` | Equals |
| `neq` | `summary.population.persons.neq.0` | Not equals |
| `gt` / `gte` | `summary.population.persons.gt.10000` | Greater than (or equal) |
| `lt` / `lte` | `medians.age.lt.30` | Less than (or equal) |
| `between` | `medians.age.between.25,40` | Between (inclusive) |
| `in` | `summary.population.persons.in.100,200,300` | In set |

**Multiple filters** (AND logic): separate with `;`

```
?filter=summary.population.persons.gte.10000;medians.age.lt.40
```

## Sorting

```
?sort=summary.population.persons.desc
?sort=medians.age.asc
```

## Pagination

Data list endpoints use `limit` and `offset`. Response includes `meta.pagination`:

```json
{
  "meta": {
    "pagination": {
      "total": 2310,
      "limit": 25,
      "offset": 0,
      "has_more": true
    }
  }
}
```

## Errors

Data endpoints return a consistent error shape:

```json
{
  "error": {
    "type": "invalid_request_error",
    "code": "invalid_parameters",
    "message": "A human-readable description of what went wrong.",
    "param": "lat"
  }
}
```

Error types: `authentication_error`, `invalid_request_error`, `validation_error`,
`not_found_error`, `rate_limit_error`, `api_error`.

Validation errors include field-level detail:

```json
{
  "error": {
    "type": "validation_error",
    "code": "invalid_parameters",
    "message": "Request validation failed.",
    "errors": [
      {"field": "lat", "message": "value is not a valid float"},
      {"field": "lng", "message": "ensure this value is less than or equal to 180"}
    ]
  }
}
```

get.description:
Returns the current health status and version of the API.

This endpoint does not require authentication. When the sync daemon is
enabled, includes per-source sync health in the response. When the request
arrives via a known API domain (or a Studio health URL is configured),
proxies Studio's health status.

Returns sync daemon health, optionally filtered to a single source.

Use ``?source=qld_traffic`` to get a single source's status — ideal for
keyword monitoring (match ``"status":"ok"``).  Omit the parameter to get
all sources with a top-level aggregate status.

Returns 503 when the sync daemon is not enabled, 404 for unknown sources.

Resolve a latitude/longitude point to its containing statistical boundaries.

Returns all boundary types (SA1-SA4, LGA, postcode, suburb, etc.) that contain the
given point, along with H3 hexagonal grid indices at resolutions 3-9 and any
matching planning regions.

Uses an H3 crosswalk table for fast lookups with a spatial query fallback.

parameters.name: types

schema.description: Comma-separated boundary types to return (e.g. `sa2,lga`). Returns all types if omitted.
schema.title: Types

parameters.description: Comma-separated boundary types to return (e.g. `sa2,lga`). Returns all types if omitted.

200.description: Matching boundaries, planning regions, and H3 indices for the given point.

get.description:
List all available boundary types grouped by country.

Returns type codes, human-readable labels, and country codes for all
supported boundary geographies (20 Australian + 20 US types).

200.description: Available boundary types grouped by country.

post.description:
Resolve multiple locations to their containing boundaries in a single request.

Accepts up to 100 locations. Each location is resolved independently.

200.description: Boundary results for each location in the batch.

post.description:
Export specific boundaries by ID as a GeoJSON FeatureCollection.

Accepts up to 500 boundary IDs. Returns a streamed GeoJSON FeatureCollection
with geometry, bbox, centroid, and properties for each boundary.

200.description: GeoJSON FeatureCollection of the requested boundaries.

get.description:
List all boundaries of a given type.

Use `contained_by` to answer spatial questions like "what SA1s are in postcode 2000?"

**Examples:**
- `GET /v1/data/boundaries/sa2` — list all SA2s
- `GET /v1/data/boundaries/sa1?contained_by=poa:2000` — SA1s in postcode 2000
- `GET /v1/data/boundaries/sa2?contained_by=lga:17200` — SA2s in Sydney LGA
- `GET /v1/data/boundaries/lga?search=melbourne` — LGAs matching "melbourne"
- `GET /v1/data/boundaries/poa?code_prefix=40` — postcodes starting with 40

parameters.name: contained_by

schema.description: Spatial filter: parent boundary_id (e.g. `poa:2000`). Returns child boundaries whose centroid falls within.
schema.title: Contained By

parameters.description: Spatial filter: parent boundary_id (e.g. `poa:2000`). Returns child boundaries whose centroid falls within.

parameters.name: code_prefix

schema.description: Filter by code prefix (e.g. '40' for postcodes 4000-4099).
schema.title: Code Prefix

parameters.description: Filter by code prefix (e.g. '40' for postcodes 4000-4099).

get.summary: Export all boundaries of a type as GeoJSON
get.description:
Download all boundaries of a type as a streamed GeoJSON FeatureCollection.

Uses server-side streaming to handle large result sets (e.g. ~62K SA1 boundaries)
without loading all features into memory.

**Simplification levels:**
- `full` — no simplification (analysis, data export)
- `medium` — ~100m tolerance (choropleth maps) — **default**
- `low` — ~500m tolerance (thumbnails, overview maps)

parameters.name: contained_by

schema.description: Spatial filter: parent boundary_id (centroid within parent).
schema.title: Contained By

parameters.description: Spatial filter: parent boundary_id (centroid within parent).

parameters.name: intersects

schema.description: Spatial filter: parent boundary_id (geometry intersects parent).
schema.title: Intersects

parameters.description: Spatial filter: parent boundary_id (geometry intersects parent).

get.description: Get metadata for a single boundary by type and code.

get.summary: Export a single boundary as GeoJSON
get.description:
Download a single boundary as a GeoJSON Feature with full geometry.

Default simplification is `full` (no simplification) for single boundaries.

List all available demographic topics.

Topics group related census fields into categories like `age`, `income`,
`housing`, etc. Use topic IDs with the `?topics=` parameter on the
demographics data endpoint to request all fields in a topic at once.

**16 standard topics** (available at all geographies):
`summary`, `age`, `income`, `housing`, `education`, `employment`,
`transport`, `family`, `language`, `ancestry`, `religion`,
`birthplace`, `health`, `internet`, `volunteering`, `unpaid_work`

**9 Indigenous Profile topics** (ILOC/IARE/IREG only):
`ip_summary`, `ip_age`, `ip_language`, `ip_education`, `ip_income`,
`ip_health`, `ip_housing`, `ip_family`, `ip_employment`

**Usage with demographics endpoint:**
- `GET /v1/data/demographics?boundary_id=sa2:117011325&topics=income` — all income fields
- `GET /v1/data/demographics?lat=-33.87&lng=151.21&topics=age,income` — multiple topics
- `POST /v1/data/demographics` with `"topics": "summary"` — polygon query with topic expansion

data.label: Key Statistics
data.description: Population totals, medians, and headline figures.

data.label: Income
data.description: Personal, family, and household income brackets.

data.label: Housing
data.description: Dwelling structure, tenure, mortgage, and rent.

data.label: Education
data.description: School completion, qualification level, field of study.

data.label: Indigenous Profile Summary
data.description: Key stats for Indigenous geographies (ILOC/IARE/IREG only).

get.description:
List all available derived percentage fields.

Derived fields are computed at query time from raw census data. Use them
with `?fields=derived.pct_indigenous` on the demographics endpoint.

**63 static fields**, always available, no extra params needed. Covers:
- **Population** (G01): indigenous, born overseas/australia, english/other language,
  citizen, children, working age, seniors, education attendance, school completion
- **Age brackets** (G01): 11 fields for each G01 age bracket (0-4 through 85+)
- **Motor vehicles** (G34): no car, 1/2/3+ cars
- **Dwelling structure** (G42): separate house, semi-detached, flat/apartment, other
- **Labour force** (G43): unemployed, full/part-time, in/not in labour force
- **Qualifications** (G43): postgrad, bachelor, grad diploma, adv diploma, cert III/IV, I/II
- **Migration** (G43): moved 1yr/5yr ago, same address
- **Household income** (G33): negative/nil, under $650, $1k-2k, $2k+/week
- **Rent** (G40): under $200, $200-449, $450+/week
- **Mortgage** (G38): under $600, $600-999, $1000+/month
- **Household composition** (G35): lone person, large (5+), family household

Get detailed metadata for a single field, including related fields.

**Field types:**

- **Count fields** (`unit: "count"`) — raw census counts (e.g. `summary.population.persons`).
  Use directly with `?fields=` on the demographics endpoint.
- **Median fields** (`unit: "years"`, `"dollars_weekly"`, etc.) — pre-computed medians
  from the ABS (e.g. `medians.age`, `medians.personal_income_weekly`).
- **Derived percentage fields** (`unit: "percentage"`, `is_derived: true`) — computed at
  query time as `(numerator / denominator) * 100`. Request with `?fields=derived.pct_indigenous`.
  Returns a value between 0 and 100. 63 static fields covering population, age brackets,
  housing, employment, qualifications, and more.
  Use `GET /v1/data/demographics/derived` to list all available fields with labels.

**Dimensions:**

Fields with `dimensions` like `{"sex": "female", "age_min": 25, "age_max": 34}` are
used by segment queries. Add `&sex=female&age_min=25&age_max=34` to a demographics
request to get a cross-tab breakdown filtered to that segment.

**Mixing field types:**

You can request regular counts, medians, and derived percentages together:
```
?fields=summary.population.persons,medians.age,derived.pct_indigenous
```

count_field.description: A raw count field. Use with `?fields=summary.population.persons` on the demographics endpoint.

median_field.description: A pre-computed median. Unit tells you how to interpret the value.

derived_percentage.description: Computed at query time from raw census counts. Request with `?fields=derived.pct_indigenous`. Returns a percentage (0-100).

segment_field.description: Fields with `sex`/`age_min`/`age_max` dimensions are used by segment queries. Add `&sex=female&age_min=25&age_max=34` to filter.

get.description:
Browse and search the demographic field catalog.

Use `topic` to filter by category, `search` for free-text matching.
The catalog contains 13,498 fields across 25 topics.

**Common workflows:**

1. **Discover what's available:** `GET /v1/data/demographics/fields?topic=income`
2. **Search by keyword:** `GET /v1/data/demographics/fields?search=median`
3. **Get field detail:** `GET /v1/data/demographics/fields/medians.age`
4. **Use in a query:** `GET /v1/data/demographics?boundary_id=sa2:117011325&fields=medians.age`
5. **Use derived percentages:** `GET /v1/data/demographics?lat=-33.87&lng=151.21&fields=derived.pct_indigenous,derived.pct_seniors_65_plus`

parameters.name: search

schema.description: Search fields by name or label (e.g. `median`, `population`).
schema.title: Search

parameters.description: Search fields by name or label (e.g. `median`, `population`).

get.description:
Unified demographics endpoint. Combine parameters to express what you want.

**Point lookup:** `?lat=-33.87&lng=151.21` (defaults to SA1, use `boundary_type` to change)

**Single boundary:** `?boundary_id=sa2:117011325`

**Multiple boundaries:** `?boundary_id=sa1:123,sa1:456` (add `&aggregate=true` to aggregate)

**Browse by type:** `?boundary_type=sa2` (paginated, supports filter/sort/contained_by)

**Segment query:** Add `sex`, `age_min`, `age_max` to any single-boundary query with a `topics` value.
Returns `segment_pct` (percentage of boundary population in the segment).

parameters.name: boundary_type

schema.description: Boundary type for point resolution or browse mode (e.g. `sa2`, `lga`).
schema.title: Boundary Type

parameters.description: Boundary type for point resolution or browse mode (e.g. `sa2`, `lga`).

parameters.name: topics

schema.description: Comma-separated topic IDs (e.g. `age,income`). Use `all` for everything. Default: `summary`.
schema.title: Topics

parameters.description: Comma-separated topic IDs (e.g. `age,income`). Use `all` for everything. Default: `summary`.

200.description: Demographic data (shape varies by query mode).

post.description:
Query demographic data for boundaries intersecting a GeoJSON polygon.

Draw a polygon on a map and get demographics for the area.
Returns either individual boundary results (with `overlap_fraction`) or
area-weighted aggregated statistics (`aggregate: true`).

Accepts the same field/topic/output-control parameters as the GET endpoint
for schema parity. Segment filters (`sex`, `age_min`, `age_max`) are not
supported for polygon queries and will return 400 if set.

Use `topics` or `fields` to control which data is returned.
Use `limit`/`offset` to paginate non-aggregate boundary results.

200.description: Demographic data for boundaries intersecting the polygon.

polygon_individual.description: Non-aggregate polygon query returns each intersecting boundary with its overlap fraction.

polygon_aggregate.description: Area-weighted aggregate. Count fields are multiplied by overlap_fraction; medians use population-weighted averages.

post.summary: Enrich locations with boundary and datapack data
post.description:
Batch-enrich locations with boundary resolution and datapack data.

For each location, resolves the containing boundaries and attaches enrichment
data from the requested datapacks.

**Datapacks available**: `demographics`, `fundraising`.

**Limits**: Up to 100 locations per request.

**Example request body**:
```json
{
  "locations": [
    {"id": "loc1", "location": {"lng": 151.2093, "lat": -33.8688}}
  ],
  "resolve": ["sa2", "lga"],
  "enrich": [{"datapack": "demographics", "fields": "population.total"}],
  "output_boundary": "sa2"
}
```

200.description: Enriched location results with resolved boundaries and attached datapack data.

get.description:
List and filter real-time incidents.

Supports hierarchical filtering: `type` (incident category), `event_type` (specific
event category), `event_subtype` (granular subtype), plus status, state, severity,
source, boundary intersection, radius search, and time range.

Both `type` and `status` accept comma-separated values for multi-value filtering
(e.g. `?status=active,upcoming` or `?type=traffic,fire`).

**Ordering**: Radius queries default to closest-first (`sort=distance`) and include
`distance_km` in each result. Use `sort=newest` to revert to newest-first ordering.
Non-spatial queries always order by newest first.

Time range filters: `since`/`until` filter on `started_at`, `ends_after`/`ends_before`
filter on `expected_end_at` (useful for finding planned outages within a window).

Each incident includes structured location fields: `locality` (suburb/area name),
`road_name` (affected road), and `location_detail` (free-text location description).

Set `detail=true` to include full geometry, description, and raw source data per
incident (same fields as the single-incident detail endpoint).

parameters.name: type

schema.description: Filter by incident type (traffic, fire, power, weather). Comma-separated for multiple.

parameters.description: Filter by incident type (traffic, fire, power, weather). Comma-separated for multiple.

schema.description: Filter by event subtype (e.g. flash_flood, planned_roadworks, multi_vehicle).
schema.title: Event Subtype

schema.title: Radius Km

parameters.description: Search radius in km (requires lat, lng).

schema.description: Sort order: 'distance' (closest first) or 'newest' (most recent first). Default: 'distance' for radius queries, 'newest' otherwise.
schema.title: Sort

parameters.description: Sort order: 'distance' (closest first) or 'newest' (most recent first). Default: 'distance' for radius queries, 'newest' otherwise.

200.description: Paginated list of incidents. Use detail=true for full geometry and source data.

data.title: Unplanned power outage — PADDINGTON, RED HILL

get.description:
List all available incident data sources.

Each source represents a government or utility data feed (e.g. QLD Traffic,
QFD Fire, Energex/Ergon Outages, BOM Weather). Includes sync status information.

data.label: QLD Traffic
data.description: Real-time traffic incidents from QLD Traffic

get.description:
Get aggregated incident counts by type hierarchy.

Returns counts grouped by `incident_type` → `event_type` → `event_subtype`.
Supports the same filters as the list endpoint (type, status, severity, spatial, time range)
but without pagination — the response is a single summary object. Both `type` and `status`
accept comma-separated values (e.g. `?status=active,upcoming`).

The response includes two top-level keys:

- **`types`** — filtered counts matching the current query parameters.
- **`catalog`** — an **unfiltered** list of every `incident_type` / `event_type` /
  `event_subtype` combination that exists in the database. This is always the full
  set regardless of filters, so UIs can render complete filter menus (including
  categories with a zero count in the current view).

parameters.name: type

schema.description: Filter by incident type (traffic, fire, power, weather). Comma-separated for multiple.

get.description:
Get full detail for a single incident.

Includes geometry, raw source data, structured location fields (`locality`,
`road_name`, `location_detail`), and full description with impact details.

200.description: Full incident detail including geometry and source data.

data.title: Unplanned power outage — PADDINGTON, RED HILL

post.description:
Search for incidents intersecting a GeoJSON geometry.

Send a GeoJSON Polygon or MultiPolygon in the request body. Optionally filter
by incident type, status, and severity. Both `type` and `status` accept
comma-separated values (e.g. `"status": "active,upcoming"`). Results include
structured location fields (`locality`, `road_name`, `location_detail`).

**Ordering**: Defaults to closest-first (`sort: "distance"`) based on the polygon
centroid, with `distance_km` in each result. Use `sort: "newest"` for newest-first.

Set `detail: true` in the body to include full geometry, description, and
raw source data per incident.

200.description: Incidents intersecting the provided geometry. Use detail=true for full data.

data.title: Unplanned power outage — PADDINGTON, RED HILL

get.description:
Generate Meta Ads targeting circles from boundary polygons.

Decomposes one or more statistical boundaries into a set of inclusion
(positive) and exclusion (negative) circles that approximate the boundary
shape for use with Meta Ads Manager custom location targeting.

**Caching:** Results are cached by boundary hash. Repeat requests return
instantly from cache regardless of boundary size. Pass `nocache=true` to
force recomputation.

**Async for large boundaries:** Boundaries with total area > 100 km² are
automatically offloaded to a background worker. The endpoint returns 202
with a `poll_url` to check progress.

**How it works:**

1. Fetches and unions the requested boundary geometries
2. Analyses shape complexity (concavity ratio)
3. Auto-sizes the circle radius based on total area and complexity
4. Generates inclusion circles via grid sampling inside the boundary
5. Generates exclusion circles for overshoot areas outside the boundary
6. Ensures total circles stay within Meta's 200-location limit

**Output formats:**

- `json` (default): structured response with inclusion/exclusion arrays
- `csv`: downloadable CSV with type, latitude, longitude, radius, distance_unit columns

**Limits:**

- 1–20 boundary IDs per request
- Combined output capped at 200 circles (Meta's limit)
- Radius range: 1.0–80 km (0.62–50 miles)

parameters.name: boundary_id

schema.description: Comma-separated boundary IDs to target (e.g. 'sa2:117011325,lga:17200'). Max 20.
schema.title: Boundary Id

parameters.description: Comma-separated boundary IDs to target (e.g. 'sa2:117011325,lga:17200'). Max 20.

parameters.name: mode

schema.description: Targeting mode: 'circles' (default) or 'postal' (ZIP/postcode inclusion + exclusion circles).

get.description:
Poll the status of a targeting job.

Returns the job status, and the result when completed.
Jobs are account-scoped — you can only access jobs created by your account.

200.description: Current status and result of a targeting job.

get.summary: Vector tile for a boundary layer
get.description:
Serve MVT vector tiles for any boundary type.

**URL template:** `/v1/tiles/{layer}/{z}/{x}/{y}.pbf?apikey=YOUR_KEY`

Returns a protobuf-encoded Mapbox Vector Tile containing boundary polygons
that intersect the requested tile. Each feature includes properties:
`boundary_id`, `name`, `state`, and (for flood layers) `flood_risk` and `flood_type`.

Responses are cached for 1 hour and gzip-compressed when accepted.
Empty tiles return 204 No Content.

parameters.name: layer

schema.description: Boundary type to serve as tile layer. AU: `sa1`–`sa4`, `lga`, `poa`, `sal`, `ced`, `sed`, `gccsa`, `ste`, `sua`, `ucl`, `ra`, `sos`, `sosr`, `iloc`, `iare`, `ireg`, `flood`. US: `us_state`, `us_county`, `us_tract`, `us_bg`, `us_place`, `us_cousub`, `us_cd`, `us_sldu`, `us_sldl`, `us_cbsa`, `us_csa`, `us_unsd`, `us_elsd`, `us_scsd`, `us_aiannh`, `us_region`, `us_division`, `us_zcta`, `us_ua`, `us_puma`.
schema.title: Layer

get.description:
This endpoint returns all locations in the account.

The results can be filtered by:
  • Map ID
  • Layer ID
  • External ID
  • Nearest: Returns up to the 24 closest locations from a provided latitude and longitude or address and travel profile ("driving", "cycling", "walking"). This can be sorted by time or distance. Note: If the source location is on water or inaccessible, the server will respond with the nearest locations by direct line and not include a duration or distance.
Example request: ``` curl --location --request GET 'https://api.mapulus.com/api/v1/locations' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --header 'Authorization: Bearer ' \ --data '{ "map_id": "", "nearest": { "lat": "-27.52851640781164", "lon": "152.97921775875503", "sort_by": "time", "profile": "driving", "limit": "10" } }'

parameters.name: nearest[address]
parameters.description: Address for nearest filter (must have either a lat/lon or an address)

parameters.name: nearest[sort_by]
parameters.description: Sort by "distance" or "time" for nearest filter

parameters.name: nearest[profile]
parameters.description: Profile for routing ("driving", "cycling", "walking") for nearest filter

parameters.name: nearest[live_traffic]
parameters.description: Include live traffic ("true" or "false") for nearest filter

parameters.name: nearest[limit]
parameters.description: Limit the number of locations returned for nearest filter. Max 24.

200.description: Succcess: Returns all locations in account.

401.description: Unauthorized: Invalid API Key or does not have the correct perimission to access resource.

items.example: You are not authorized to perform this action

422.description: Unprocessable Entity: The request because the request was well-formed, but for example the map_id or layer_id is not valid.

post.description:
This endpoint creates a single location when provided a valid layer_id in the JSON body. Locations must also have either a valid address or latitude and longitude. When adding `custom_attributes`, if the custom_attribute does not already exist on the layer (based on name), you can set the `create_or_update_custom_attributes` in the main body of the JSON or `create_or_update_custom_attribute` in the nested attribute object to create or ignore it. Without this flag, new custom_attributes will return an error.
Example request:
```
curl --location 'https://api.mapulus.com/api/v1/locations' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--header 'Authorization: Bearer <your-token>' \
--data '{
  "lat": -27.4704528,
  "lon": 153.0260341,
  "label": null,
  "title": "Head Office",
  "address": "Brisbane, Australia",
  "external_id": null,
  "travel_contour": true,
  "contour_mode": "driving",
  "contour_metric": "minutes",
  "contour_style": "polygons",
  "contour_minutes": "15",
  "layer_id": "<layer-id>",
  "create_or_update_custom_attributes": true,
  "custom_attributes": [
    {
      "object": "custom_attribute",
      "name": "Location Type",
      "value": "Public Store",
      "create_or_update_custom_attribute": false
    }
  ]
}'

422.description: Unprocessable Entity: The request because the request was well-formed, but the custom attribute does not exist.

items.example: Custom attribute 'Location Type' not found on the defined layer. You can automatically create or ignore the missing attributes by adding the `create_or_update_custom_attributes` flag in the main body or `create_or_update_custom_attribute` on the individual custom attribute.

delete.description:
This endpoint delete a single location.
Example request:
```

--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--header 'Authorization: Bearer <your-token>' \

put.description:
This endpoint updates a location. Locations must also have either a valid `address` or `lat` and `lon`. When adding `custom_attributes`, if the custom_attribute does not already exist on the layer (based on name), you can set the `create_or_update_custom_attributes` in the main body of the JSON or `create_or_update_custom_attribute` in the nested attribute object to create or ignore it. Without this flag, new custom_attributes will return an error. Moving the location to new layers is possible by passing a new `layer_id` in the JSON body.
Example request:

```
curl --location --request PUT 'https://api.mapulus.com/api/v1/locations/<location-id>' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--header 'Authorization: Bearer <your-token>' \
--data '{
  "object": "location",
  "lat": -27.4704528,
  "lon": 153.0260341,
  "label": null,
  "title": "Head Office",
  "address": "Brisbane, Australia",
  "external_id": null,
  "travel_contour": true,
  "contour_mode": "driving",
  "contour_metric": "minutes",
  "contour_style": "polygons",
  "contour_minutes": "15",
  "layer_id": "0b942634-814f-42f7-82f5-7f4d06213275",
  "create_or_update_custom_attributes": true,
  "custom_attributes": [
    {
      "object": "custom_attribute",
      "name": "Location Type",
      "value": "Public Store",
      "create_or_update_custom_attribute": false
    }
  ]
}'

IncidentSummaryResponse.title: IncidentSummaryResponse
IncidentSummaryResponse.description:
Aggregated incident counts grouped by incident_type → event_type → event_subtype.

The ``types`` array contains counts matching the current filters.
The ``catalog`` array is **always unfiltered** — it lists every
incident_type / event_type / event_subtype combination that has ever
appeared in the database, regardless of the query filters applied to
``types``. This is useful for building UI filter toggles that show all
possible categories (including those with a zero count in the current view).

IncidentTypeCatalog.title: IncidentTypeCatalog
IncidentTypeCatalog.description: Known event_type → subtypes for an incident_type (for UI toggles).

tags.name: Boundaries
tags.description: Australian statistical boundaries: point lookup, spatial queries, and boundary listing.

tags.name: Demographics
tags.description: Query demographic data (ABS Census) by boundary. Supports topic expansion, field selection, filtering, sorting, and aggregation.

tags.name: Enrich
tags.description: Batch-enrich locations with boundary resolution and datapack data (demographics, fundraising).

tags.name: Incidents
tags.description: Real-time traffic, fire, power outage, and weather incidents with spatial queries.

tags.name: Targeting
tags.description: Generate ad platform targeting circles from boundary geometry. Small boundaries compute synchronously; large boundaries (>100 km²) are offloaded to async jobs with polling. Results are cached by boundary hash.

tags.name: Tiles
tags.description:
Mapbox Vector Tiles (MVT) for boundary layers. Supports `?apikey=` for map library tile URL templates.

**Available layers:**

| Layer | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `sa1` | Statistical Area Level 1 (finest ABS mesh block aggregation) |
| `sa2` | Statistical Area Level 2 (suburbs / localities) |
| `sa3` | Statistical Area Level 3 (regional groupings) |
| `sa4` | Statistical Area Level 4 (labour market regions) |
| `lga` | Local Government Areas |
| `poa` | Postal Areas (postcodes) |
| `sal` | Suburbs and Localities |
| `ced` | Commonwealth Electoral Divisions |
| `sed` | State Electoral Divisions |
| `gccsa` | Greater Capital City Statistical Areas |
| `ste` | States and Territories |
| `sua` | Significant Urban Areas |
| `ucl` | Urban Centres and Localities |
| `ra` | Remoteness Areas |
| `sos` | Section of State |
| `sosr` | Section of State Range |
| `iloc` | Indigenous Locations |
| `iare` | Indigenous Areas |
| `ireg` | Indigenous Regions |
| `flood` | Brisbane flood zones (risk levels + flood types) |

**Tile properties:** `boundary_id`, `name`, `state`, `flood_risk`, `flood_type`
